_note: Zarma (zar) — values are English placeholders pending professional translation.

meta.title: Muriyar Ta
meta.description: Amplifying girls' voices through anonymous storytelling, digital media, and advocacy.

podcast.metaDescription: Anonymous stories, expert conversation, and the topics communities rarely discuss aloud.
podcast.heroBody: Anonymous stories from girls and young women, brought together with expert voices, taboo-breaking conversations, and roundtables with the next generation — across Niger and West Africa.
podcast.featuredEmptyTitle: A featured episode will appear here soon
podcast.featuredEmptyBody: When the first episodes are published, one will be highlighted here as a starting point.
podcast.seriesDescription: Four editorial series, each with a distinct purpose.
podcast.themesDescription: Topics the podcast explores. New themes appear here as episodes are tagged.
podcast.themesEmpty: Themes will appear here as episodes are published.
podcast.languagesDescription: Listen in the language closest to home.
podcast.latestEmptyBody: When the first episodes are published, they'll show up here, newest first.

anonymous-voices.name: Anonymous Voices
anonymous-voices.tagline: One anonymous story, in her own words.
anonymous-voices.description: Our flagship series. Each episode tells one anonymous story, carefully de-identified and shared with consent.

beyond-the-story.name: Beyond the Story
beyond-the-story.tagline: An anonymous story, with experts to help us understand.
beyond-the-story.description: Each episode begins with an anonymous story, then brings together psychologists, lawyers, teachers, researchers, and activists to ask why this happens — and what we can do.

breaking-the-silence.name: Breaking the Silence
breaking-the-silence.tagline: Conversations on topics communities rarely discuss aloud.
breaking-the-silence.description: Child marriage, menstruation, consent, mental health, body image, puberty, domestic violence, friendship — talked about openly, with care.

youth-voices.name: Youth Voices
youth-voices.description: Roundtables between young people on education, leadership, university, marriage, social media, dreams, and community change.

about.metaDescription: Muriyar Ta exists to create a safe and anonymous space where girls and young women can share their stories in their own words.
about.heroTitle: Every story deserves to be heard.
about.heroBody: Behind every statistic is a person with dreams, fears, hopes, and experiences that deserve to be acknowledged. Muriyar Ta exists to create a safe and anonymous space where girls and young women can share their stories in their own words because lasting change begins by listening.
about.storyPara1: Before the world knew Malala Yousafzai as a Nobel Peace Prize laureate and global advocate for girls' education, she was simply a young girl sharing her experiences of being denied the right to learn. Her story did not introduce a new problem to the world. Girls had been excluded from education for generations. What changed was that people finally connected with the human experience behind the statistics.
about.storyPara2: Muriyar Ta was born from the same belief.
about.storyPara3: Too often, conversations about child marriage, gender based violence, barriers to education, and harmful social norms are reduced to numbers, reports, and policy discussions. While statistics help us understand the scale of a problem, they can never fully capture the realities of the people living through it.
about.storyPara4: Behind every statistic is a story waiting to be heard.
about.storyPara5: Muriyar Ta exists so that those stories are no longer left unheard.
about.anonymityPara1: Sharing deeply personal experiences should never come at the cost of someone's safety.
about.anonymityPara2: For many girls and young women, speaking openly about child marriage, gender based violence, discrimination, family pressure, or other sensitive experiences can expose them to stigma, retaliation, or further harm. Too often, silence is not a choice. It is a survival strategy.
about.anonymityPara3: By allowing stories to be shared anonymously, Muriyar Ta removes some of those barriers. We believe girls should be able to speak honestly without fear of judgment or consequences. Anonymity allows contributors to remain protected while ensuring their experiences can still educate, inspire, and contribute to meaningful conversations.
about.missionBody: To create a safe and anonymous platform where girls and young women can share their stories freely, without fear, stigma, or judgment. Through storytelling, digital media, and advocacy, Muriyar Ta amplifies voices that too often go unheard, helping transform lived experiences into awareness, dialogue, and action. Rather than speaking on behalf of girls and young women, Muriyar Ta exists to create a space where they can speak for themselves.
about.visionBody: A world where every girl has the opportunity to safely share her story, have her voice heard, and contribute to shaping the policies, programs, and social norms that affect her life.
about.namePara1: Muriyar Ta means "Her Voice" in Hausa. More than a name, it is a promise. A promise that every girl who visits this platform will find a space where her voice matters, and her story will help shed light on the injustices faced by girls and women.
about.founderPara1: Growing up in Maradi, Niger, I often questioned the silence surrounding issues that shaped the lives of girls and young women. I was curious, constantly asking questions about traditions, expectations, and topics that were rarely discussed openly.
about.founderPara2: From a very young age, I was exposed to community work and leadership experiences that transformed my understanding of advocacy. They taught me that meaningful change begins by listening to the people most affected.
about.founderPara3: Why do we so rarely hear directly from the girls whose lives are being discussed?
about.founderPara4: Too often, their experiences are summarized through statistics, reports, and research while their own voices remain absent. Muriyar Ta was born from the belief that every statistic represents a real person with a story worth hearing. I wanted to create a space where girls and young women could share those experiences safely, anonymously, and in their own words. A space where they are not spoken for, but listened to.
about.founderPara5: As someone passionate about gender equality, education, international development, and social impact, I believe storytelling is more than a way of sharing experiences. It is a way of building understanding, challenging harmful norms, and informing better policies and solutions.
about.pullQuote: Silence protects perpetrators, not victims. Speaking up is the only way to break the silence and the cycle of violence.
about.ctaBody: Every story shared is a step toward breaking silence, building understanding, and creating change. Join us in building a future where every girl feels safe to be heard.

partner.metaDescription: Muriyar Ta collaborates with organizations, researchers, educators, media, and donors advancing the rights and voices of girls and young women.
partner.heroBody: Muriyar Ta grows through collaboration. If your work advances the safety, education, or voice of girls and young women in Niger and across West Africa, there is a place for you here.
partner.whyPara1: Lasting change is never the work of a single organization. Ending child marriage, expanding access to education, and confronting gender-based violence require coalitions — of survivors, communities, researchers, educators, and institutions that can act at scale.
partner.whyPara2: Partnership is how we turn stories into policy, awareness into action, and isolated efforts into shared momentum. We work with partners who bring rigor, care, and a commitment to centering the voices of the people most affected.
partner.audiencesDescription: We welcome collaboration with a wide range of organizations and individuals.

ngos.title: NGOs
ngos.body: Local, national, and international organizations working on gender equality, education, and child protection.

researchers.title: Researchers
researchers.body: Academics and independent researchers studying storytelling, gender, education, and public health in West Africa.

education.title: Educational Institutions
education.body: Schools, universities, and training programs integrating survivor-centered storytelling into curricula and outreach.

donors.title: Foundations & Donors
donors.body: Foundations, philanthropists, and grant-makers investing in gender equality and youth empowerment.

media.title: Media
media.body: Journalists, podcasters, and outlets committed to responsible reporting on sensitive issues.

volunteers.title: Volunteers
volunteers.body: Translators, editors, designers, and community members lending time and skills to the mission.

partner.collaborationsDescription: Every partnership is shaped by what each side brings. These are the areas where we most often work together.

outreach.title: Community Outreach
outreach.body: Reaching girls and young women where they live, learn, and gather.

research.title: Research
research.body: Joint studies, data sharing under strict ethical safeguards, and evidence generation.

podcasts.title: Podcasts
podcasts.body: Co-produced episodes, guest interviews, and cross-promotion with aligned shows.

workshops.title: Workshops
workshops.body: In-person and online sessions on storytelling, digital safety, and advocacy.

advocacy.title: Advocacy Campaigns
advocacy.body: Joint campaigns amplifying survivor voices in policy and public dialogue.

resources.title: Resource Development
resources.body: Guides, toolkits, and educational materials produced collaboratively.

translation.title: Translation
translation.body: Making our platform and content accessible in Hausa, Zarma, French, and beyond.

grants.title: Grant Partnerships
grants.body: Applying jointly for funding that supports shared projects and long-term impact.

partner.prioritiesDescription: Areas where we're actively looking for partners in the months ahead.

priorities.regional: Partnerships across Niger and West Africa

partner.processDescription: A straightforward path from a first message to meaningful collaboration.

reach.title: Reach Out
reach.body: Send a message describing your organization and what you'd like to explore.

talk.title: Let's Talk
talk.body: We schedule an initial conversation to understand your goals and share ours.

explore.title: Explore Opportunities
explore.body: Together we identify where our missions overlap and what a partnership could look like.

build.title: Build Meaningful Impact Together
build.body: We formalize the collaboration and begin the shared work.

partner.ctaBody: If any of this speaks to your work, we'd love to hear from you. Send us a message and we'll take it from there.

report.metaDescription: Confidentially report a concern to the Muriyar Ta team.
report.heroSubtitle: If you or someone you know is experiencing child marriage, gender-based violence, barriers to education, or another serious concern, you can safely share information here.
report.noticeNotEmergency: This is not an emergency service.
report.noticeCallEmergency: If someone is in immediate danger, contact local emergency services right away.
report.noticeModerationReview: Reports may be reviewed by the Muriyar Ta moderation team.
report.formDescription: Only the fields marked required are needed. Add any detail you feel comfortable sharing.
report.descriptionHelp: Explain what happened in your own words. Up to 8,000 characters.
report.emailHelp: Only used if the moderation team needs to reach you.
report.phoneHelp: Only used if the moderation team needs to reach you.
report.consentLabel: I understand this report may be reviewed by the Muriyar Ta moderation team.
report.successBody: Your report has been received. The Muriyar Ta team will review it and, if you provided contact information, may follow up.

errors.genericFailure: Something went wrong. Please try again.

report.supportDescription: If you need help right away, these direct resources are available.

report.supportCrisisBody: Immediate-help resources for people experiencing crisis or in danger.

contact.metaDescription: Get in touch with the Muriyar Ta team.
contact.heroDescription: Whether you're a survivor, researcher, journalist, NGO, educator, or potential partner, we'd love to hear from you.
contact.formDescription: The Muriyar Ta team reads every message. Please share a bit about who you are and what you'd like to talk about.
contact.consentLabel: I understand this message may be reviewed by the Muriyar Ta team.
contact.successBody: Your message has been received. The Muriyar Ta team will read it and reply as soon as we can.

errors.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
errors.consentRequired: Please confirm your acknowledgement to submit.

contact.channelsDescription: For specific requests, you can email us directly at the address that fits your needs.

general.title: General inquiries
general.body: Questions, feedback, or anything that doesn't fit elsewhere.

partnerships.title: Partnerships
partnerships.body: NGOs, educational institutions, and community organizations looking to collaborate.

media.title: Media
media.body: Journalists, podcasters, and press requesting interviews or comment.

research.title: Research
research.body: Researchers and academics interested in the platform, its data, or its methodology.

contact.faqDescription: A few of the questions we get most often.

anonymity.a: Yes. You can share a story anonymously via the Submit page and contact us here without providing personal details beyond a name and email.

response_time.q: How long does a response take?
response_time.a: We try to reply within a few working days. Complex requests, especially research or partnership discussions, may take longer.

partner.q: Can organizations partner with Muriyar Ta?
partner.a: Yes. We work with NGOs, educational institutions, and community groups across West Africa. Reach out via the Partnerships channel to start a conversation.

research.a: Researchers can email us at the Research channel with a short description of their project. We review requests carefully to protect the people who share their stories with us.

contact.footerNote: Muriyar Ta is committed to protecting the safety and dignity of the people who share their stories with us. Please do not include identifying details about others in your message.

footer.crisisBody: This platform reviews stories asynchronously and cannot respond to emergencies. If you are in danger, contact local emergency services or a support organization.
footer.mission: A safe, anonymous space where girls and young women can share their stories, be heard, and help shape change.
footer.contactBody: NGOs, researchers, educators, and policymakers are welcome to reach out.
footer.tagline: Inspiring advocacy and change through lived experiences.
